text:
Market today: Mixed/cautious tone. Inflation signals are contradictory (Goolsbee hawkish, Williams dovish), which keeps rate-sensitive tech choppy. The bigger real driver for your book: Iran tightening the Strait of Hormuz spikes oil, raises input costs, and adds geopolitical risk — exactly the kind of macro shock that hits growth/tech multiples hard when you're already on margin.

Your holdings: Three need attention. PLTR is down -17.56% and headlines confirm it's having its worst month in years — if it keeps sliding toward your $85 stop, that's a real margin hit. AVGO is your largest position, down -13.18%; soaring component-cost headlines (see Xbox/Apple) suggest margin pressure across semis, which doesn't help. SPAL is down -16.49% — this is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily; losses compound fast, especially on margin. Review size now.

Watch: 1) Oil prices — a sustained spike tightens financial conditions and reprices tech. 2) AVGO price action near $336 stop — it's your biggest exposure.

Bottom line: Margin plus three positions bleeding double-digits in a geopolitical risk-on environment means your stop discipline on PLTR, AVGO, and SPAL is the only thing protecting you right now.
